summary: Do not ship. The committed RED witness and verify record correctly pin the real pass-abort symptom, but the new load-bearing W12 cfg/link closure is both self-failing and bypassable. This is a new downward-closure defect, not the registered M18 class. Simpler alternative: replace the raw substring rules with one token-aware Rust syntax scan. Open question: none.

findings.title: W12's raw-text closure gate cannot safely reach GREEN
findings.body: W12 scans every `src/store/**/*.rs` file after removing only comments, but its own implementation contains the forbidden needles as string literals (`"#[cfg(not("`, `"cfg!("`, `"cfg_attr"`, `"#[path"`, and `"include!("`). Because W12 lives in `src/store/mod.rs`, every zero-count assertion matches at least its own needle and fails. Splitting those literals alone is insufficient: valid Rust spellings such as `#[cfg (not(test))]`, `cfg ! (test)`, `#[ path = "..."]`, and `include !("...")` evade the exact substring checks, reopening the production-skew and link-redirection classes W12 claims to close. An engineer following the plan either gets permanently stuck or weakens the gate into an unsafe GREEN.
findings.recommendation: Replace W12 with a Rust lexer/parser or token-normalized scan that ignores comments and literal contents while recognizing attributes and macro invocations independent of whitespace. Verify it scans `mod.rs` itself and add spaced-spelling mutants for every forbidden construct.

next_steps: Correct W12 and add self-scan plus whitespace-variant witnesses.
Update B-1 implementation and acceptance text to explicitly require W12/W1–W12 before rerunning the plan gate.
